skill.name: executing-plans
skill.description: Execute a written implementation plan in controlled task batches with review checkpoints and verification steps.
skill.summary: Runs implementation plans in batches, reports progress, and pauses for review at key checkpoints.
skill.author: obra

security_audit.summary: The static finding for a weak cryptographic algorithm at SKILL.md:3 is a false positive. That line is front matter describing review checkpoints and contains no cryptographic code, command execution, network access, secret access, or prompt injection attempt.

content.value_statement: Long implementation plans can drift when work is done without structured review. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through batched execution, verification, and feedback checkpoints.
actual_capabilities: Loads and critically reviews a written implementation plan before starting work.
Executes the first three planned tasks by default before pausing for review.
Tracks task progress from in progress to completed during each batch.
Runs the verification steps defined by the plan for each task.
Reports implemented changes and verification output at batch checkpoints.
Stops and asks for help when blockers, unclear steps, or repeated failures appear.
limitations: Requires an existing written implementation plan with actionable steps.
Does not create the original architecture or product plan by itself.
Depends on the available project tools and referenced skills.
Pauses instead of guessing when instructions or verification results are unclear.

use_cases.title: Execute a scoped feature plan
use_cases.description: Follow a prepared feature plan in small batches, verify each task, and report progress for review.

use_cases.title: Control multi-step refactoring work
use_cases.description: Apply a refactor plan carefully while preserving checkpoints for review and correction.

use_cases.title: Coordinate agent handoff sessions
use_cases.description: Use a written plan to continue work across sessions without losing review discipline.

prompt_templates.title: Run a simple plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner use with a clear plan file.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the executing-plans skill to execute the plan in docs/implementation-plan.md. Review the plan first, then start the first batch if there are no concerns.

prompt_templates.title: Execute with review after each batch
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the executing-plans skill for this implementation plan. Complete the default first batch, run the listed verifications, then stop and report results for review.

prompt_templates.title: Continue after plan feedback
prompt_templates.scenario: Resume work after the plan was revised.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the executing-plans skill to continue from the updated plan. Re-review the changed sections, identify any new concerns, and then execute the next batch.

prompt_templates.title: Manage a complex handoff
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced use for a longer plan across sessions.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the executing-plans skill to take over this implementation plan from a previous session. Verify current progress, reconcile completed tasks, execute the next batch, and stop at the next checkpoint.

output_examples.input: Execute the first batch from the payment refactor plan.
output_examples.output: The assistant reviews the plan, flags no blockers, completes the first three tasks, runs the listed checks, and reports the completed work.

output_examples.input: Continue after I updated the migration plan.
output_examples.output: The assistant re-reads the changed plan, notes any concerns, resumes at the next unfinished task, and pauses after the batch for feedback.

output_examples.input: The verification step failed twice during the batch.
output_examples.output: The assistant stops execution, reports the failing verification, summarizes the attempted fixes, and asks for clarification before continuing.

best_practices: Provide a written plan with small tasks and explicit verification steps.
Review each checkpoint before asking the assistant to continue.
Update the plan when feedback changes the intended implementation path.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill without a concrete implementation plan.
Do not ask the assistant to skip verification steps listed in the plan.
Do not continue through blockers when the skill has stopped for clarification.

faq.question: What kind of plan does this skill need?
faq.answer: It works best with a written implementation plan that has ordered tasks, concrete steps, and verification instructions.

faq.question: Does it write the plan for me?
faq.answer: No. It is designed to execute an existing plan, not to create the plan from scratch.

faq.question: How many tasks does it run before review?
faq.answer: The default first batch is three tasks, unless the plan or user specifies a different batch size.

faq.question: What happens if a task fails verification?
faq.answer: The skill instructs the assistant to stop, report the issue, and ask for help instead of guessing.

faq.question: Can it continue after feedback?
faq.answer: Yes. It applies needed changes, executes the next batch, and repeats checkpoints until the plan is complete.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The reviewed file contains workflow guidance only and no executable code, network access, or secret handling.
